title: Ai full-stack engineer
description:
### **summary**


at place, we don’t just experiment with ai — we ship it. we build ai\-powered products that make a real impact, from automating marketing workflows to intelligent lead routing and smart assistants. our tools help the top real estate teams across north america grow and scale.


we’re looking for an experienced senior full\-stack software engineer who’s excited about building full products that leverage ai. we primarily work with javascript (node.js) and a bit of python, so familiarity with these technologies is a plus. more importantly, we want someone who can turn innovative ideas into tools that thousands of real estate professionals rely on.


you’ll collaborate with our product, design, and platform engineering teams to bring impactful ai\-driven features to life, all while using a modern tech stack you’re comfortable with

### **responsibilities**

**what you’ll do**

* **build full\-stack ai features:** use javascript, node.js, and python to integrate ai capabilities into our web applications. you’ll be building real\-time features that enhance the way real estate professionals work.
* **own end\-to\-end development:** from backend apis to frontend ux, you’ll deliver features that blend ai seamlessly into our core workflows like crm, transactions, and marketing.
* **build and integrate ai agents:** work on creating and integrating ai agents that utilize large language models and vector databases to help agents move faster and smarter.
* **scale \& deploy:** collaborate with our devops team to ensure our services are secure, scalable, and built on modern infrastructure like aws, docker, and kubernetes.
* **collaborate cross\-functionally:** partner with product managers, designers, and other stakeholders to shape the roadmap and deliver impactful features.

### **qualifications**

### **what we’re looking for**

**required:**

* 3\+ years experience building web applications (frontend \+ backend).
* solid programming skills in **node.js**, **python**, or similar.
* experience working with frontend frameworks like **react**, **next.js**, or similar.
* comfortable shipping production code, writing tests, and deploying to cloud infrastructure.

**nice to have:**

* experience using llm apis (openai, claude, etc.) in production systems.
* familiarity with langchain, crewai, or other agent frameworks.
* worked with vector databases like pinecone or weaviate.
* interest or experience in real estate tech or b2b saas.

### **about\-our\-team**

**required:**

* 3\+ years of experience building web applications, both frontend and backend.
* solid programming skills in node.js, python, or similar languages.
* experience with frontend frameworks like react, next.js, or similar.
* hands\-on experience incorporating ai into your development workflow, especially using agentic development tools or building ai\-powered features.

title: Senior Cloud Software Engineer (Full-Stack)
description:
description:
**overview**


tanaq technical services (tts), a division of st. george tanaq (sgt)corporation, is an 8(a) alaskan native small business that specializes in delivering enterprise integrated technology solutions and support services to the federal government. tts’s innovative approach combines proactive strategies, efficient processes, and emerging technologies to deliver seamless, integrated services for our clients. tts prides itself in being mission driven, people focused. to learn more about us, visit https://tanaq.com/tanaq\-technical\-services.

**about the role**


we are seeking a **senior cloud software engineer** to provide advanced application support for cloud\-based application development, modernization, and implementation in support of the u.s. department of housing and urban development (hud) office of the chief information officer (ocio).


the senior cloud software engineer supports hud’s enterprise modernization strategy by designing, developing, and deploying secure, scalable cloud applications. this role will deliver end\-to\-end features across ui, services, data, and integrations, while working within hud’s ppm life cycle, agile delivery practices, and ai readiness to improve customer experience (cx) and mission outcomes.


this is a fully remote position supporting a federal government contract that requires a federal background check and naci clearance. candidates must reside in the united states.

**responsibilities**

**cloud application development**

* build and maintain full\-stack, cloud\-hosted applications (frontend, backend, apis, integrations).
* lead end\-to\-end solution design, coding, code reviews, and integration for enterprise applications.
* develop prototypes, proofs of concept (pocs), and pilots; mature successful solutions to production scale.
* produce and maintain technical documentation (designs, test plans, architecture diagrams, deployment/runbooks) in approved repositories.
* ensure secure coding, automated testing, ci/cd, and alignment with 508 accessibility and cx validation.
* collaborate closely with ux, data, devops, security, and program management teams; support briefings/demos and technical reviews.

**cloud infrastructure \& devsecops**

* automate infrastructure provisioning using infrastructure\-as\-code (iac) tools such as terraform, cloudformation, or arm templates.
* design and implement ci/cd pipelines to support automated software development and deployment workflows.
* integrate security testing and compliance checks into devsecops pipelines (sast, dast, dependency scanning).

**enterprise modernization support**

* support migration and modernization of legacy systems to cloud environments.
* build and modernize full stack applications using cloud native architectures, integrating modern front end frameworks with scalable backend services and apis.
* collaborate with enterprise architects to implement scalable platform services supporting ai and advanced analytics initiatives.

**security \& compliance**

* ensure cloud solutions meet federal cybersecurity standards including nist 800\-53, fisma, and zero trust architecture principles.
* implement secure identity and access management integrations aligned with eicam and zero trust requirements.
* support security monitoring, logging, and compliance reporting within cloud environments.

**collaboration \& agile delivery**

* work within agile development teams supporting iterative delivery of hud modernization initiatives.
* collaborate with product owners, data architects, cybersecurity teams, and business analysts to translate requirements into technical solutions.
* contribute technical documentation, architecture diagrams, and system design artifacts.


requirements:
**required experience and skills**

* 8\+ years of full\-stack development with cloud platforms (aws/azure/gcp).
* front\-end development expertise designing and coding user interfaces with frameworks react.js and react \+ typescript.
* back\-end development experience writing server\-side logic using node.js, python (django/flask), java (spring boot), or .net and managing databases postgresql, sql, nosql and ensure secure data handling.
* strong proficiency in modern programming languages such as java, python, go, or node.js.
* cloud integration experience using cloud\-native services like serverless functions, container orchestration (kubernetes, docker), and managed databases. deploying applications to cloud platforms (aws, azure, gcp).
* api and service management experience building and integrating restful or graphql apis and connecting third\-party services (payment gateways, authentication providers, etc.).
* experience with containerization technologies using docker, kubernetes, or openshift.
* experience implementing devsecops pipelines and ci/cd automation.
* knowledge of federal frameworks including 508, nist, fisma, and fedramp.
* experience calling ai/ml apis (azure openai, aws bedrock, google vertex, etc.).
* **ability to pass required federal background screening and obtain and maintain both government clearance and customer approval.**
* **must be legally authorized to work in the united states without the need for employer sponsorship, now or at any time in the future.**

**preferred qualifications**

* previous experience in regulated/federal government environments is a plus.
* knowledge of ai/ml infrastructure readiness and platform engineering.
* experience building applications that use large language models (llms), embeddings, vector search, chat/completion models.
* experience integrating ai into front\-end and back\-end workflows, integrating ai/ml apis into applications, familiarity with llms, embeddings, and rag architectures, and understanding of ai governance, responsible ai, and data handling constraints
* familiarity with identity and access management solutions supporting zero trust architectures.

**technical skills**

* languages: javascript/typescript, python, java
* frontend: react (preferred), angular, or vue
* backend: node.js, spring boot, .net core, or django/fastapi
* architectures: restful apis, microservices, event\-driven patterns
* data: sql/nosql (postgresql, sql server, dynamodb/mongodb), api design/integration
* cloud/platform: serverless (lambda/functions), containers (docker, kubernetes), iac (terraform/arm).
* ci/cd: azure devops, gitlab ci, jenkins; test automation frameworks.
* security: authn/z, secrets management, secure coding practices

**key competencies**

* problem solving, systems thinking, and quality mindset.
* clear written/oral communication with technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
* collaboration and accountability in a fast\-paced agile environment.
* bias for documentation, knowledge transfer, and continuous improvement.

**education and certifications**

* bachelor’s degree in computer science, software engineering, information systems, or related technical field (or equivalent experience).
* aws certified solutions architect – associate/professional preferred.
* aws certified developer – associate, microsoft certified: azure developer associate, or ckad preferred.

**physical requirements**

* prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer. may need to lift up to 25 pounds occasionally.

**who we are**


tanaq technical services (tts) strives to deeply understand and analyze our clients’ vision, needs, and requirements so we may provide alternative solutions, empowering them to choose the best resolution. we aim to achieve excellence by delivering on our commitments to our clients, employees, and partners.

**our commitment to non\-discrimination**


tanaq technical services is an equal employment opportunity employer. all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to disability, status as a protected veteran or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, or local law. tanaq complies with the drug\-free workplace act of 1988 and e\-verify.


if you are an individual with a disability and need assistance completing any part of the application process, please email accommodation@tanaq.com to request a reasonable accommodation. this email is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.

**notice on candidate ai usage**


tanaq is committed to ensuring a fair and competitive interview process for all candidates based on their experience, skills and education. to ensure the integrity of the interview process, the use of artificial intelligence (ai) tools to generate or assist with responses during phone, in person and virtual interviews is not allowed. however, candidates who require a reasonable accommodation that may involve ai are required to contact us prior to their interview at accommodation@tanaq.com.

**to apply for this and other positions with our company, visit:**


https://recruiting.paylocity.com/recruiting/jobs/all/31dd0607\-5fe1\-43cc\-a916\-35084f250a54/tanaq\-technical\-services\-llc

title: Backend/AI Engineer
description:
**left main rei** is a top 1% salesforce partner company primarily serving real estate investors and commercial real estate brokers. we specialize in providing a robust salesforce solution optimized for real estate industry business processes and have harnessed the power of data \+ ai to help our customers move faster, think smarter, and achieve more.

we’re a team of passionate innovators on a mission to transform how real estate businesses operate and scale. if you’re excited about shaping the future of a dynamic industry, this is your moment.

**job details**

we’re looking for a **backend / ai engineer** to help us design, build, and evolve the backend services, data infrastructure, and ai\-powered capabilities behind the next generation of left main products.

this role will sit at the intersection of backend engineering, cloud infrastructure, data platforms, and applied ai. you will help build scalable apis, data pipelines, event\-driven workflows, and genai\-enabled product experiences that support initiatives across left main crm, dealsignals, disposignals, data insights, enrichment, and future ai\-powered products.

you’ll work closely with product, data, salesforce, and engineering leaders to move more intelligence into our aws and snowflake architecture, create reliable services that integrate with salesforce and external data providers, and prototype new genai use cases that can become production\-ready product capabilities.

this is a hands\-on individual contributor role with meaningful architectural influence. we’re looking for someone who is energized by experimentation, comfortable owning production systems, and excited to help define the future of our data \+ ai tech stack.

**job responsibilities**

* design, build, maintain, and optimize backend services and restful apis that connect left main products, salesforce, snowflake, aws, and external data providers.
* develop and operate serverless and cloud\-native systems using aws services such as lambda, api gateway, s3, iam, cloudwatch, eventbridge, kinesis, and related services.
* build reliable data\-processing workflows for real estate data ingestion, enrichment, scoring, subscriptions, matching, deduplication, and delivery back into customer\-facing applications.
* design scalable snowflake\-backed workflows, including sql transformations, stored procedures, snowpark or python\-based processing, and performance optimization.
* architect and implement genai and ai\-powered product capabilities, including role\-based agents, retrieval\-augmented generation, prompt workflows, ai\-assisted analysis, customer\-facing copilots, and internal productivity tools.
* prototype new ai use cases quickly, evaluate technical feasibility, and help move the most promising experiments into secure, reliable, production\-ready services.
* collaborate with salesforce engineers to design integration patterns using platform events, apis, async processing, and well\-defined data contracts between salesforce, aws, and snowflake.
* support predictive and ml\-enabled use cases such as lead scoring, buyer scoring, property intelligence, recommendation workflows, anomaly detection, and customer insights.
* improve reliability, observability, and operational maturity across backend systems through structured logging, monitoring, alerting, automated tests, and clear incident response practices.
* contribute to ci/cd, infrastructure automation, environment management, and deployment practices for backend and data services.
* bring ideas, technical curiosity, and product\-minded thinking to roadmap discussions, architecture reviews, and innovation planning.

**requirements**

* 2\+ years of professional backend engineering experience building production\-grade apis, services, data workflows, or cloud\-native systems.
* strong hands\-on experience with python for backend development, scripting, automation, data processing, or ai/ml workflows.
* experience building restful apis and integrating systems through apis, webhooks, event\-driven patterns, or message\-based architectures.
* strong working knowledge of aws, especially serverless or cloud\-native services such as lambda, api gateway, s3, iam, cloudwatch, eventbridge, sqs, sns, kinesis, or similar technologies.
* strong sql skills and experience working with modern data warehouses, preferably snowflake.
* experience designing systems that are reliable, secure, observable, cost\-conscious, and maintainable.
* familiarity with modern ai and genai development patterns, such as llm apis, prompt engineering, rag, embeddings, vector databases, model evaluation, agent workflows, or ai\-assisted automation.
* ability to use modern ai development tools such as chatgpt, claude, cursor, github copilot, or similar tools to improve development speed, code quality, documentation, and problem solving.
* experience working with ci/cd pipelines, github actions, automated testing, infrastructure\-as\-code, or deployment automation.
* comfort working in a fast\-moving startup environment where priorities evolve and engineers are expected to own outcomes, not just tickets.
* strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to partner across product, engineering, data, salesforce, and leadership teams.

**benefits:**

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* dental insurance
* schedule
* health insurance
* life insurance
* paid time off
* vision insurance
* flexible remote work arrangements
* opportunities for professional growth and development
* collaborative and inclusive work culture
* salesforce certification incentives

**compensation**:

* $100,000\-$140,000 per year
* generous benefit package
* paid holidays

title: Software & AI Engineer
description:
universe force group is hiring a software \& ai engineer to join our engineering team and help us build intelligent, scalable, production\-ready software systems.

we are looking for an individual who can think, architect, build, test, ship, and improve ai\-powered products that solve real business problems.

this is a fully remote role for someone who wants to work on advanced ai systems, business automation, enterprise platforms, intelligent agents, igaming technology, and digital transformation projects across international markets.

**about universe force group**

universe force group is a technology company focused on ai, software engineering, system integration, digital transformation, and intelligent business solutions.

we build practical technology for companies that need more than advice. we design, develop, integrate, and deliver software that helps businesses automate operations, improve efficiency, scale faster, and compete in a market increasingly driven by ai.

our work covers areas such as:

* ai\-powered business automation
* enterprise software platforms
* llm\-based systems and intelligent agents
* crm, erp, and workflow automation
* igaming and digital platform technology
* custom web, mobile, and backend systems
* cloud, api, and system integration projects

**about the role**

as a software \& ai engineer at universe force group, you will work closely with our cto, to design and build ai\-driven systems from concept to production.

the role includes building ai assistants, agentic workflows, automation systems, rag pipelines, backend services, data\-driven platforms, internal tools, and client\-facing digital products.

you will be expected to take ownership, understand the business problem behind the technical task, and deliver clean, scalable, reliable solutions.

**key responsibilities**

design, develop, and maintain ai\-powered software solutions using modern engineering practices.

build and integrate llm\-based systems, ai agents, workflow automation, apis, and backend services.

work with models, embeddings, vector databases, prompt engineering, rag systems, and ai orchestration frameworks.

develop scalable backend systems and integrations with third\-party platforms, crms, databases, and business tools.

convert business and client requirements into clear technical solutions.

support the architecture and development of production\-ready ai products.

improve system architecture, code quality, security, performance, scalability, and maintainability.

deploy, monitor, and support ai solutions in production environments.

work closely with product, business, and engineering teams across multiple projects.

mentor other developers and contribute to technical decision\-making.

stay up to date with emerging ai engineering tools, frameworks, and best practices.

**requirements**

5\+ years of professional software engineering experience.

strong backend development experience with node.js, python, typescript, or similar technologies.

practical experience working with ai/ml systems, llms, openai apis, anthropic, langchain, llamaindex, vector databases, rag systems, or agentic workflows.

strong understanding of apis, microservices, databases, system architecture, and cloud\-based software.

experience with databases such as postgresql, mongodb, redis, or similar.

experience with cloud platforms such as aws, azure, google cloud, or similar.

strong understanding of clean code, security, scalability, performance, and production deployment.

ability to work independently in a fully remote environment.

strong problem\-solving mindset and ownership mentality.

clear communication skills and ability to work in a fast\-moving business environment.

**nice to have**

bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, software engineering, artificial intelligence, data science, or a related technical field.

experience with ai agents, multi\-agent systems, workflow orchestration, and automation platforms.

experience with rag pipelines, vector search, document intelligence, and enterprise ai tools.

experience with devops, docker, ci/cd, kubernetes, and cloud infrastructure.

experience in igaming, fintech, hr tech, crm systems, enterprise automation, or digital transformation projects.

frontend experience with react, next.js, or flutter.

understanding of data privacy, security, compliance, and responsible ai implementation.

experience working with international teams and remote\-first delivery.

**what we offer**

a fully remote position with flexibility and ownership.

opportunity to work on advanced ai and software products with real commercial use cases.

direct involvement in architecture, product direction, and technical decisions.

a fast\-moving environment where strong engineers can grow quickly and take responsibility.

exposure to international projects across global markets.

work with a team focused on ai, software engineering, automation, and digital transformation.

flexible cooperation model depending on experience, seniority, and location.

growth within the company.

**who we are looking for**

we are looking for an individual who can operate with agility, responsiveness, independence, discipline, and technical confidence.

someone who understands that ai engineering is not just about connecting apis, but about building reliable systems around intelligence, automation, data, security, and business logic.

the ideal candidate is proactive, practical, sharp and able to move from problem to solution without needing everything explained three times.

you should be comfortable working remotely, taking ownership, communicating clearly, timely and effectively, and delivering high\-quality work in a serious engineering environment.

apply here or at office@universeforcegroup.com

pay: up to $110,474\.00 per year

title: Java Full Stack Developer
description:
choosing capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world.


**location**
------------


this is a hybrid role (3 days onsite in a week) from locations such as new york, atlanta\-ga, chicago\-il, dallas\-tx or nashville\-tn


**about the job you're considering**
------------------------------------


we are seeking an **ai‑enabled full stack engineer** to build scalable, production\-ready applications using modern backend and frontend technologies. the role emphasizes **ai‑assisted development**, strong engineering fundamentals, and collaboration in an agile environment.


the ideal candidate will leverage **ai tools (copilot, cursor ai)** as a productivity accelerator while ensuring high standards of code quality, security, and performance.


**your role**
-------------


**backend development**


* develop applications using java 21 and spring boot
* build reactive apis with spring webflux (mono/flux)
* implement secure services using oauth 2\.0 / jwt
* manage builds using maven
* write unit tests using junit 5, mockito, stepverifier
* follow test\-driven development (tdd) practices
  

**frontend development**


* build dynamic uis using angular and typescript
* use rxjs for reactive programming
* develop responsive layouts using html5 and css3
* integrate frontend with backend via rest apis
  

**devops \& deployment**


* use docker for containerization
* deploy applications on kubernetes
* maintain ci/cd pipelines and git workflows
* define configurations using yaml
  

**ai‑driven development**


* use github copilot, cursor ai for code generation and debugging
* build and contribute to ai\-powered systems
* maintain a technology\-agnostic mindset
  

**quality, security \& observability**


* implement secure apis using spring security
* maintain api documentation using openapi / swagger
* monitor applications with prometheus / opentelemetry
* ensure high code quality and test coverage
  

**collaboration \& engineering discipline**


* participate in code reviews and agile ceremonies
* work across frontend, backend, and product teams
* take ownership of features end\-to\-end
* continuously learn and adapt to new tools and architectures
  

**required skills**


* java, spring boot, rest apis
* angular, typescript, rxjs
* git, ci/cd basics
* unit testing (junit, mockito)
* tdd concepts
* api security (oauth/jwt)
  

**preferred skills**


* spring webflux \& spring security
* docker, kubernetes
* observability tools
* openapi / swagger
* angular testing (jasmine/jest)
* state management (ngrx)
  

**soft skills**


* strong communication skills
* team collaboration and code review participation
* ownership and accountability
* problem\-solving ability
* adaptability and learning mindset. \#li\-ss1

title: Statistical Modeling & NPW-PTSD Framework Intern
description:
statistical modeling \& npw\-ptsd framework intern

**organization:** outlets for hope, inc. \| international truth \& trauma institute (itti)  
**program:** global peace \& conflict resolution program residency internship  
**position title:** statistical modeling \& npw\-ptsd framework intern  
**internship type:** this is a pro bono educational internship/residency opportunity intended for mentorship, professional development, research exposure, and academic internship/practicum support where applicable.  
**location:** remote  
**start date:** immediate — may 2026

overview

join the international truth \& trauma institute (itti) as a statistical modeling \& npw\-ptsd framework intern and contribute to innovative trauma\-informed analytical frameworks focused on collective trauma, governance instability, conflict exposure, displacement, violence trends, psychosocial stress systems, and national stabilization research.

this internship offers an exciting opportunity for aspiring researchers, statisticians, data scientists, and ai\-oriented analysts interested in applying advanced statistical modeling, machine learning, and computational research methods to real\-world humanitarian, peacebuilding, and mental health challenges.

interns will support the development and refinement of emerging analytical models related to trauma burden indicators, conflict destabilization patterns, population\-level psychosocial distress systems, and the npw\-ptsd (national post\-war ptsd) framework. the role integrates elements of statistical analysis, natural language processing, predictive modeling, research translation, and ai\-assisted framework development.

students seeking academic internship, residency, practicum, or school\-hour completion opportunities are strongly encouraged to apply.

responsibilities

* assist in developing and refining statistical and predictive models related to collective trauma, conflict exposure, governance instability, and psychosocial stress indicators
* support analytical work involving large\-scale humanitarian, behavioral, governance, conflict, or observational datasets
* contribute to implementation of machine learning and unsupervised learning techniques for pattern discovery, clustering, classification, and anomaly detection
* assist with interpretation and translation of statistical findings into operational, policy, and research\-informed language
* support natural language processing (nlp) projects involving reports, testimony archives, policy documents, clinical narratives, media content, or survey\-based data
* collaborate with interdisciplinary teams contributing to trauma observatory development, analytical dashboards, and peacebuilding frameworks
* assist with data mining, model validation, visualization, and algorithm optimization processes
* support cloud\-based analytical workflows and scalable computing environments where applicable
* help prepare technical reports, briefing documents, visual presentations, and analytical summaries for both technical and non\-technical audiences
* maintain organized documentation and provide structured daily work/activity reports

preferred qualifications

* graduate student or senior undergraduate student in:
* statistics
* data science
* computer science
* public health analytics
* epidemiology
* economics
* neuroscience
* quantitative social sciences
* artificial intelligence
* information systems
* electrical engineering
* applied mathematics
* strong analytical reasoning and research skills
* familiarity with programming and analytical tools such as:
* python
* r
* matlab
* sql
* spss
* tableau
* power bi
* c\+\+
* bash/linux environments
* exposure to machine learning frameworks such as:
* tensorflow
* pytorch
* spark
* hadoop
* related ai/ml tools
* understanding of:
* statistical modeling
* predictive analytics
* nlp techniques
* data mining
* unsupervised learning
* algorithm development
* familiarity with aws or other cloud computing environments is desirable
* interest in trauma\-informed systems, humanitarian analytics, peacebuilding research, governance systems, or mental health innovation strongly preferred
* ability to work independently while remaining responsive to supervision and collaboration
* strong written communication and technical documentation skills

internship requirements

* must be able to begin this month (may 2026\)
* minimum commitment:
* 3 days per week
* minimum 6 hours per workday
* must possess:
* stable high\-speed internet access
* secure laptop/computer suitable for analytical and research work
* strong initiative, professionalism, and accountability
* ability to work mostly independently while participating in collaborative research initiatives
* daily work/activity reports required for every workday completed

what interns will gain

* hands\-on experience in trauma\-informed analytics and observatory\-style modeling initiatives
* exposure to interdisciplinary applications of ai, machine learning, and statistical systems in humanitarian and peacebuilding contexts
* experience contributing to globally oriented research and policy initiatives
* mentorship within an innovative trauma\-informed research and international development environment
* portfolio\-building opportunities involving analytics, predictive modeling, framework development, and technical reporting
* documentation support for academic internship, practicum, or residency hour requirements

pay: $1\.00 per hour

work location: remote

title: Senior Python Developer
description:
role summary we are looking for an ai native development architect to design and guide the build of cloud\-native, data\- and ai\-driven applications on aws. you will define target architectures, enable engineering teams with reusable patterns and reference implementations, and accelerate delivery using modern ai\-assisted development tools.


**key responsibilities:**
* define end\-to\-end architecture for ai\-native products, including application, data, integration, security, and operations on aws.
* lead design reviews and provide technical direction across python and c\#/.net codebases.
* architect data pipelines and analytical workloads using pyspark and aws glue; establish standards for data quality, lineage, and observability.
* design and implement scalable apis and microservices using fastapi (and/or .net web apis) with clear contracts, versioning, and performance slas.
* establish reference architectures for llm/rag\-enabled capabilities (e.g., retrieval patterns, prompt management, evaluation, guardrails) aligned with organizational policies.
* partner with security, platform, and devops teams to implement secure\-by\-design practices (iam, secrets, network controls, encryption, threat modeling).
* define ci/cd, branching, testing, and release practices; improve developer productivity with automation and paved\-road templates.
* champion ai\-assisted engineering workflows using tools such as github copilot, cursor, and claude ai while ensuring code quality and compliance.
* mentor engineers, create technical documentation, and drive adoption of best practices across teams.

**required skills primary skills:** python : strong hands\-on experience building services and data workloads using python, pyspark, aws glue, and fastapi. c\#/.net : ability to design and review .net services and libraries; familiarity with modern .net runtime and patterns. aws : strong understanding of aws architecture fundamentals (networking, iam, compute, storage, managed services) and designing for scale, reliability, and cost. ai native development tools proficiency using ai coding assistants to accelerate development while maintaining engineering rigor: github copilot, cursor, claude ai. ability to establish team guidelines for ai\-assisted coding (review standards, secure prompting, ip/compliance awareness, and validation/testing). **preferred qualifications:** experience designing genai solutions (rag, tool/function calling, agents) and implementing evaluation/monitoring approaches. experience with infrastructure as code (e.g., cloudformation/cdk/terraform) and container platforms (docker/ecs/eks). knowledge of mlops patterns (model lifecycle, feature stores, experiment tracking) and data governance concepts. strong understanding of observability practices (logs/metrics/traces) and sre\-oriented reliability design. **soft skills \& competencies:** architecture leadership: can balance short\-term delivery with long\-term platform thinking. clear communication: can translate complex technical decisions for engineering and business stakeholders. hands\-on mindset: comfortable prototyping and jumping into code to unblock teams. quality and security focus: promotes testing discipline, secure coding, and operational readiness. collaboration and mentorship: builds alignment, coaches engineers, and scales best practices across squads. **what success looks like (first 90 days):** established reference architectures and coding standards for ai\-native services; improved delivery throughput via ai\-assisted workflows; delivered at least one production\-ready blueprint (api \+ data pipeline) on aws with strong security, observability, and cost controls.
**for applications and inquiries, contact:** hirings@openkyber.com

title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
**senior data scientist** 

i need someone with clinical experience preferably in laboratory settings. healthcare, pharmaceutical's, animal clinical experience is a huge plus!

if you're a perfect fit i have a start date of first week of june. email your resume to ds@dnssolutions.net

if you have a team of senior bi data analyst, senior data engineers with clinical experience. please forward this off and i'll email them the job descriptions and rates. all roles are 100% remote.

**english speaking and great communication skills is a must.** 

**job summary:**

we’re transforming how we work by harnessing the power of ai, data, and digital innovation to accelerate the discovery and development of life\-saving therapies. our vision is to embed ai and advanced analytics across every stage of our scientific and operational workflows, empowering our teams with smarter tools, seamless data, and actionable insights to deliver client\-centric services and solutions that better serve our customers and, ultimately, improve patients’ lives.

as a **senior data scientist** and a member of our enterprise data \& analytics engineering team, you will play a pivotal role in this transformation. you’ll lead the design, development, and deployment of data\-driven and ai\-powered solutions that enhance customer value, streamline operations, and create meaningful, data\-informed experiences for our employees. through advanced analytics and generative ai, you’ll help us unlock new possibilities in how we deliver science, innovation, and impact to our clients and the patients they serve.

as a **senior data scientist**, your primary **responsibilities** include:

· lead end\-to\-end data science initiatives, including data exploration, model development, validation, implementation, and support for mission\-critical applications.

· collaborate closely with cross\-functional data teams to automate data science pipelines and integrate models into business workflows and digital platforms.

· partner with business units in a consultative and collaborative manner, advising on the most effective approaches to leverage data science and generative ai technologies to deliver innovative, high\-impact solutions.

**key elements**

we are looking for the following **minimum qualifications** for this role:

· master’s with a minimum of 5 years’ experience or bachelor’s with a minimum of 7 years’ experience in data science, computer science, statistics, operations research, applied mathematics, applied physics or any related field;

· excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills with the ability to approach challenges creatively

· strong communication skills to translate the findings into actionable business recommendations through rich data visualization;

· familiar with agile methodologies and tools;

· self\-driven and capable of working both independently and collaboratively within a team setting.

· experience in or strong understanding of the life science industry, particularly in leveraging data science to support operational excellence, is preferred.

**technical expertise required:** 

· minimum of 5 years’ hands\-on experience processing, analyzing and modeling large and complex data sets.

· minimum of 3 years’ experience with the full machine learning product lifecycle, with a demonstrated ability to take initiative and ensure proactive delivery of project milestones.

· minimum of 5 years’ experience working with development toolsets such as sql, pyspark, python, pandas or scala.

· experience with generative ai technologies for scientific document reading, summarization, and generation, including prompt engineering, retrieval pipelines, and llm fine\-tuning for domain\-specific applications.

**role specific information:**

· **location**: remote

* annual bonus based on performance;

· **schedule**: monday to friday during business hours;

* permanent position as of the hiring, full\-time 40hrs per week.

pay: $139\.00 \- $148\.00 per hour

work location: remote

title: Senior API Engineer (Python)
description:

seeking a senior ai / ml \& generative ai architect to serve as a technical and strategic leader across enterprise ai initiatives. this individual will own end to end ai solution architecture, development, and deployment, with a strong emphasis on azure\-native platforms, data infrastructure, and location\-aware (gps/geospatial) intelligence. this role is ideal for a hands on architect who can design, build, and operationalize ai/ml and genai solutions, while partnering with business and engineering teams to move ideas into production quickly. must be comfortable working with onshore and offshore delivery teams.

* design and implement traditional ml, advanced analytics, and genai solutions (llms, embeddings, retrieval augmented generation, copilots, intelligent assistants)
* translate business use cases into scalable, production ready ai architectures
* define standards and best practices for model development, evaluation, deployment, and governance
* **azure platform \& data architecture** architect and deploy ai solutions primarily within an azure based ecosystem
* leverage azure ai foundry, azure openai, azure ml, and supporting azure services
* design and optimize data pipelines and feature stores using: databricks snowflake oracle redis
* ensure architectures are secure, performant, scalable, and cost efficient

* support use cases related to store locations, logistics, supply chain, fleet, customer movement, and location aware insights
* integrate geospatial data with enterprise analytics and ai models to drive real world decisioning
* **development \& deployment (hands on)** build and review production quality code, primarily in python
* support both architecture and development, including: model training and experimentation api/service design ci/cd and mlops practices
* drive deployment into production using azure native and cloud aligned deployment patterns
* **collaboration \& delivery** work effectively with offshore development teams, providing clear architectural guidance and code reviews
* partner closely with data engineers, platform teams, product owners, and business stakeholders
* serve as a technical mentor and thought leader within the ai practice

**required skills \& experience**


* **core technical skills** senior level experience in ai/ml and generative ai
* strong expertise in microsoft azure, with hands on production experience
* experience with azure ai foundry
* deep experience with: databricks snowflake oracle redis
* strong python development skills
* experience across architecture, development, and deployment (not architecture only)
* **platform \& data experience** strong understanding of cloud data platforms, modern data stacks, and ai aligned architectures
* experience designing scalable, resilient ml/ai systems in enterprise environments
* familiarity with mlops, model lifecycle management, and deployment automation
* **geospatial / gps** experience working with gps or geospatial data and integrating it into analytics or ai workflows
* ability to design location aware solutions that support operational and business use cases
* **collaboration \& leadership** comfortable working with offshore and distributed teams
* excellent communication skills; able to explain complex ai concepts to technical and non technical audiences
* proven ability to lead technically while remaining hands on

**nice to have**


experience in retail, supply chain, logistics, or field based operations


our benefits package includes: comprehensive medical benefits competitive pay 401(k) retirement plan and much more!

title: Senior Technical Engineer
description:
**\#weareparamount on a mission to unleash the power of content… you in?**  

we’ve got the brands, we’ve got the stars, we’ve got thepowerto achieve our mission to entertain the planet – now all we’re missing is… you! becoming a part of paramount means joining a team of passionate people who not only recognize the power of content but also enjoy a touch of fun and uniqueness. together, we co\-create moments that matter – both for our audiences and our employees – and aim to leave a positive mark on culture.

**in this role you’ll:**
========================

  

the senior technical engineer deploys and maintains infrastructure platforms supporting paramount's 24/7 broadcast and media operations. you'll work hands\-on with virtualization, storage, networking, and broadcast systems \- building, troubleshooting, and optimizing platforms that power creative and live production workflows. this role combines hands\-on technical work with problem\-solving: you'll deploy and maintain platforms, troubleshoot complex issues, support incident response, and contribute to a culture of innovation and continuous improvement.

**basic qualifications you bring:**
===================================

  

* bachelor's degree in computer science.
* strongly proficient in linux and windows server environments. this includes building systems, configuring them, hardening their security, and optimizing performance.
* 5\+ years of hands\-on experience deploying and supporting enterprise infrastructure platforms in production environments.

**bonus skills:**
=================

  

* hands\-on experience with virtualization platforms like nutanix or vmware is required. this includes support for deployment. it also includes configuration, administration, and troubleshooting. candidates should have experience in compute, storage, and networking layers.
* a solid knowledge of networking basics is required. this includes tcp/ip, routing, switching, vlans, load balancing, and workflows that are affected by latency.
* skilled in designing and supporting san and nas storage environments using smb and nfs for high\-throughput creative and broadcast workloads.
* demonstrated capability implementing and using monitoring, alerting, and log analysis to support anticipatory detection and root\-cause analysis (e.g., zabbix, new relic, or similar).
* experience with scripting languages such as bash, powershell, or python for automation, troubleshooting, and operational efficiency.
* hands\-on experience with cloud platforms (aws, google cloud, or similar), including vm instances, storage, networking, and hybrid connectivity for on\-premises and cloud integration.
* proven ability to support database platforms including mysql, mariadb, postgresql, or similar systems in production environments.
* proficient in supporting containerized workloads using docker and basic kubernetes concepts.
* ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously, prioritizing efficiency and delivering results with minimal supervision.
* willingness and ability to work assigned shifts, including nights, weekends, and holidays in a 24/7 operational environment if required.
* ability to provide hands\-on operational support during critical incidents or when support team capacity is constrained.
* has great interpersonal skills. works well in a team. collaborates with technical and production teams. communicates clearly with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
* ability to hoist up to 50 lbs and perform physical tasks associated with equipment installation, rack mounting, and data center operations.
* solid documentation discipline, including creation of build guides, runbooks, and operational standards.
* ai\-conscious mindset, with interest in understanding how ai\-assisted tools can support troubleshooting, automation, documentation, and operational efficiency.
* hands\-on administration of active directory, including users, security groups, and access controls is a plus.
* experience with broadcast platforms (mimir, avid, vizrt, telestream, dalet, grass valley), codecs/formats, or media infrastructure is a plus.

  

paramount skydance corporation (nasdaq: psky) is a leading global media and entertainment company that creates premium content and experiences for audiences worldwide. driven by iconic studios, networks and streaming services, paramount's portfolio of consumer brands includes cbs, showtime networks, paramount pictures, nickelodeon, mtv, comedy central, bet, paramount\+, and pluto tv, among others. paramount delivers the largest share of the u.s. television audience and boasts one of the industry's most important and extensive libraries of tv and film titles. in addition to offering innovative streaming services and digital video products, the company provides powerful capabilities in production, distribution and advertising solutions.

  

additional information

  

hiring salary range: $115,000\.00 \- 135,000\.00\.

  

the hiring salary range for this position applies to new york city, california, colorado, washington state, and most other geographies. starting pay for the successful applicant depends on a variety of job\-related factors, including but not limited to geographic location, market demands, experience, training, and education. the benefits available for this position include medical, dental, vision, 401(k) plan, life insurance coverage, disability benefits, tuition assistance program and pto or, if applicable, as otherwise dictated by the appropriate collective bargaining agreement. this position is bonus eligible.

  



**what we offer:*** attractive compensation and comprehensive benefits packages. check out our full list of benefits here: https://www.paramount.com/careers/benefits
* generous paid time off.
* an exciting and fulfilling opportunity to be part of one of paramount’s most dynamic teams.
* opportunities for both on\-site and virtual engagement events.
* unique opportunities to make meaningful connections and build a vibrant community, both inside and outside the workplace.
* explore life at paramount: https://www.paramount.com/careers/life\-at\-paramount

  

paramount is an equal opportunity employer (eoe) including disability/vet.

  

at paramount, the spirit of inclusion feeds into everything that we do, on\-screen and off. from the programming and movies we create to employee benefits/programs and social impact outreach initiatives, we believe that opportunity, access, resources and rewards should be available to and for the benefit of all. paramount is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. we are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, and veteran status.

  

if you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access https://www.paramount.com/careers as a result of your disability. you can request reasonable accommodations by calling 212\.846\.5500 or by sending an email to paramountaccommodations@paramount.com. only messages left for this purpose will be returned.

title: Job Title: Senior AI, ML, Cybersecurity & Engineering Talent | Remote | $180K+ Base

about us

* **artificial intelligence \& machine learning:** ai engineers, ml engineers, llm engineers, nlp engineers, data scientists, mlops engineers

title: Senior AI Engineer
description:
klearnow is building the intelligence layer for global trade and logistics. we turn fragmented, messy data into instant, reliable intelligence, enabling ai to reason and act in high\-stakes, real\-world environments. we are a small, sharp team operating at the frontier, and we are looking for builders who want to define the technical foundation from the ground up.



### **role overview**



as a senior ai engineer on our applied ai team, you will design and deploy production\-grade agentic ai systems that transform real\-world workflows at scale. this role is ideal for someone who thrives on ownership, is energized by ambiguity, and wants to ship consequential systems, not just run experiments. you will own end\-to\-end ai pipelines from data ingestion through retrieval, orchestration, and output, while also shaping product direction and engineering culture.

  


### **what you’ll do**


* design and build multi\-agent systems that reason across messy, real\-world data

own end\-to\-end ai pipelines — data retrieval orchestration* output
* develop evaluation frameworks, benchmarks, and quality loops to ensure reliability
* build and scale data ingestion and retrieval infrastructure
* integrate llms with enterprise data, tools, and workflows using frameworks such as:
	+ agno, langchain, llamaindex, and semantic kernel
	+ claude code, cursor, and augmentcode for enterprise agent deployment
* ship full\-stack product features in close collaboration with end users
* help define technical architecture, product direction, and engineering culture
* write clear, well\-structured python and typescript code for production ml systems

  


### **what we are looking for**


* 4\+ years of hands\-on software engineering experience; startup experience strongly preferred
* proven track record shipping production\-grade ai systems, not just prototypes
* strong hands\-on experience with:
	+ agent architectures, llm systems, and rag pipelines
	+ retrieval, grounding, and orchestration techniques
	+ python (strong proficiency) and typescript (working knowledge)
* 4\+ years of infrastructure experience (cloud services, distributed systems)
* 4\+ years working with containerization tools such as docker and kubernetes
* 2\+ years of experience building with large language models (llms) in production
* full\-stack ownership mindset — comfortable operating across the entire system
* strong data instincts and experience working with messy, real\-world datasets
* must be authorized to work in the united states (no visa sponsorship available)
* ability to commute to the san jose, ca office (hybrid schedule)
### **nice to have**


* experience with evals frameworks and ai quality measurement
* background in ai infrastructure or distributed systems
* exposure to automotive, logistics, or other physical\-world domains
* mlops / devops experience; ci/cd pipelines, docker, kubernetes
* former founder or early employee at a startup

  


### **why this role**


* define the core ai systems of the company from the ground up
* work on problems where ai meets the physical world at scale
* high ownership, high impact, your work ships immediately
* small, sharp team with a bias for action and a frontier mindset
### **company benefits**



klearnow offers a competitive compensation package commensurate with experience, including:


* comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance
* equity participation
* flexible time off
* collaborative, hybrid in\-office environment in our san jose, ca headquarters
* the us salary range for this position is $187k to $215k in the san jose bay area, california
*klearnow is an equal opportunity employer. we celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees*

we may use artificial intelligence (ai) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. these tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. if you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Senior Insurance Data Analyst
description:
at orion180, we don’t just stand out for what we do; we shine because of how we do it. our mission is to deliver an exceptional insurance experience through innovative technology, unparalleled customer service, and a comprehensive suite of products. our vision is to be the premier global provider of insurance solutions and redefine the insurance industry.

  

orion180 is proud to call three vibrant cities home. our headquarters on florida’s stunning space coast in melbourne offers an exceptional quality of life, with affordable housing, excellent transportation, and close proximity to major cities like orlando, tampa, and miami. our irving, texas, office places us in a dynamic center of culture, business, and entertainment, keeping us closely connected to innovation and growth. our draper, utah, location, nestled in the heart of the salt lake valley, connects us to a rapidly growing tech ecosystem and provides access to the outdoor lifestyle and natural beauty the region is known for.

 **join our team as a senior insurance data analyst!!**

  

we believe analytics isn't a back\-office function. it's not a reporting layer bolted onto the business after the real decisions get made. it's the foundation that tells you where risk actually lives, where growth is hiding, and where you're missing opportunities before it's too late to do anything about it.

  

that belief is driving real investment in our people, platforms, and in how deeply analytics is woven into the way we operate. from underwriting to distribution to customer experience, we're building a data culture where insight drives action.

  

it also means evolving how we think about the role of an analyst.

  

the expectation isn’t just technical execution. it’s curiosity. ownership. the ability to step into ambiguity, frame the problem, and help guide the business toward better decisions.

 ***to be eligible for this role, you must have the proper work authorizations to work in the us without sponsorship***

 **what you’ll do:**

 **data strategy \& pipeline development**

* develop and maintain reproducible, scalable data pipelines to support advanced modeling and analytics workflows.
* design and implement predictive and time\-series models using classical and machine learning methods.
* collaborate with sales, underwriting, claims, and actuarial teams to integrate models into production environments.

**modeling \& analysis**

* leverage python, sql, and statistical analysis to identify key data patterns and multivariate correlations.
* conduct exploratory data analysis and ensure data integrity across structured and unstructured datasets.
* document model assumptions, governance processes, and validation results to maintain compliance with regulatory standards.

**visualization \& communication**

* build intuitive dashboards and data visualizations using power bi that translate complex insights into clear business intelligence.
* communicate findings and recommendations to cross\-functional teams, including leadership and technical partners.
* stay current with industry trends and emerging data science tools to continuously improve analytical capabilities.

 **what we’re looking for:**

**education:**

* bachelor’s degree in statistics, data science, computer science, mathematics, or a related field

**experience:**

* proven experience in data analysis, modeling, and visualization within the insurance or financial services industry.
* proficiency in python, sql optimization, statistical modeling, api development, and forecasting techniques.
* hands\-on experience with cloud platforms such as aws, azure, or gcp and familiarity with mlops frameworks.
* strong understanding of both data science and actuarial principles.

**skills \& attributes:**

* excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and critical\-thinking skills.
* strong communication and collaboration abilities across technical and non\-technical teams.
* ability to manage multiple projects in a fast\-paced, results\-driven environment.

 ***this is position is 100% onsite in our irving, tx location***

  

we are interested in candidates who are developing capabilities aligned with modern, forward\-looking analytics practices. this includes experience or familiarity with semantic data modeling and governed metric layers, working within scalable cloud data platforms (e.g., snowflake, databricks), and integrating analytics within broader data engineering workflows. exposure to emerging capabilities such as ai\-assisted analysis, automated insight generation, and advanced data visualization techniques is highly valued.

 **join our team \& make an impact!** at orion180, we don’t just meet expectations, we exceed them. if you’re ready to take your career to the next level and be part of a growing, forward\-thinking company, apply today!

  

* dynamic environment: on\-site role with a fast\-paced and collaborative team culture. results\-driven office where your contributions make a real impact.
* compensation: competitive base pay and performance bonuses.
* career growth: mentorship, growth tracks, and professional development.
* benefits: medical, dental, vision, 401k, paid holidays, pto and more!

  

the office environment is fast\-paced and collaborative. an employee must be willing and able to work their regularly assigned work schedule onsite, and in times of need, be able to work an extended schedule depending on company or departmental needs, project requirements, or customer demands.

  

while performing general duties for this position, the employee is regularly required to sit, stand, and/or walk around (including the use of stairs). other demands include the ability to openly communicate with others by talking, listening, comprehending, and reading; being able to lift light objects (\<25 lbs); and using standard office equipment such as computers, printers, and phones. in addition, there is an occasional need to bend, twist, or squat down to open/close cabinets and reach for files or other standard office\-type objects.

  

orion180 is an equal opportunity employer. all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or protected veteran status.

  

reasonable accommodations are available for qualified individuals with disabilities during the application process. applicants who need accommodations may email hr@orion180\.com to request assistance.

title: Observatory Data Analyst Intern
description:
observatory data analyst intern

**organization:** outlets for hope, inc. \| international truth \& trauma institute (itti)  
**program:** global peace \& conflict resolution program residency internship  
**position title:** observatory data analyst intern  
**internship type:** this is a pro bono educational internship/residency opportunity intended for mentorship, professional development, research exposure, and academic internship/practicum support where applicable.  
**location:** remote  
**start date:** immediate — may 2026

job overview

join the international truth \& trauma institute (itti) as an observatory data analyst intern and become part of an innovative, trauma\-informed global research initiative focused on conflict analysis, collective trauma mapping, humanitarian intelligence, governance instability, and peacebuilding systems.

this internship offers aspiring data analysts, data scientists, and research innovators an opportunity to contribute to the development of emerging observatory\-style platforms designed to analyze conflict exposure, displacement trends, trauma burden indicators, persecution patterns, election\-related instability, and population\-level psychosocial stressors.

interns will support the development of advanced analytical systems, dashboards, and research pipelines utilizing modern data analytics approaches, machine learning concepts, visualization tools, and large\-scale data integration methods. this role is ideal for individuals passionate about leveraging data for humanitarian insight, global stabilization, public health intelligence, and conflict prevention.

students seeking academic internship, residency, practicum, or school\-hour completion opportunities are strongly encouraged to apply.

responsibilities

* assist in designing and organizing observatory\-style data pipelines and structured research datasets
* support collection, cleaning, validation, and interpretation of large\-scale conflict, governance, displacement, and trauma\-related datasets
* contribute to development of predictive and analytical models related to collective trauma, conflict exposure, violence trends, and social instability
* assist with machine learning and pattern recognition projects involving classification, clustering, anomaly detection, and trend analysis
* support database management and data organization using sql, spreadsheets, cloud\-based systems, or related platforms
* help integrate linked data sources from humanitarian, governance, conflict, and public datasets for comprehensive analysis
* participate in dashboard creation, visualization, reporting, and observatory prototype development
* assist in analyzing unstructured information sources such as reports, testimony archives, media data, policy documents, and observational logs
* support production of policy briefs, trauma observatory reports, analytics summaries, and visual presentations
* collaborate with leadership on research\-informed peacebuilding and stabilization initiatives
* maintain organized documentation and provide daily work/activity reports for each workday completed

preferred qualifications

* graduate student or senior undergraduate student in:
* data science
* computer science
* statistics
* peace \& conflict studies
* public health informatics
* economics
* international development
* information systems
* political science
* sociology
* geographic information systems (gis)
* strong analytical thinking and problem\-solving skills
* familiarity with programming or analytical tools such as:
* python
* r
* sql
* excel
* tableau
* power bi
* spss
* bash/unix shell
* exposure to big data concepts, machine learning, predictive analytics, or visualization systems is highly desirable
* familiarity with tensorflow, spark, hadoop, cloud computing environments (aws or similar), or etl/data pipeline concepts is a plus
* interest in ai\-assisted analytics, humanitarian intelligence systems, trauma\-informed policy research, or observatory development strongly preferred
* ability to work independently while remaining responsive to supervision and collaboration
* strong written communication and documentation skills

internship requirements

* must be available to begin this month (may 2026\)
* minimum commitment:
* 3 days per week
* minimum 6 hours per workday
* must possess:
* stable high\-speed internet access
* secure laptop/computer suitable for remote analytical work
* strong initiative, professionalism, and accountability
* ability to work mostly independently while contributing to collaborative initiatives
* daily work/activity reports are required for every workday completed

what interns will gain

* hands\-on experience contributing to emerging trauma observatory and peace analytics initiatives
* exposure to interdisciplinary applications of data science in humanitarian, conflict, governance, and public health settings
* opportunity to contribute to globally oriented policy and research projects
* mentorship within a mission\-driven international development and trauma\-informed systems environment
* portfolio\-building experience in analytics, observatory development, research reporting, and visualization
* documentation support for academic internship, practicum, or residency hour requirements

pay: $1\.00 per hour

work location: hybrid remote in brockton, ma 02302

title: Equity Intern
description:
the summer 2027 equity internship role is an exciting opportunity to gain practical, hands\-on equity research experience and career\-building mentorship. the individual will work closely with a small team of investment professionals and portfolio managers who employ a bottom\-up fundamental framework to assess a company's competitive advantages and are committed to a rigorous, results\-oriented investment process.





candidates should have a high degree of intellectual curiosity, excellent academic credentials, possess strong research, writing skills and effective communication skills and an interest in pursuing a career in equity investing post\-graduation. moreover, candidates should have a demonstrated ability to use primary and secondary data sources when researching investment opportunities.





bessemer's equity teams are built with the goal of providing the best platform for performance and long\-term career growth.





***the bessemer trust internship program:***



our summer internship program offers real world experience working side\-by\-side with experts from departments and offices across our firm. you will manage individual and group projects, interact with senior leaders, and attend a variety of career development sessions. you will also network with fellow interns in\-person and virtually, as well as during our intern days held in our new york headquarters.





this 10\-week program is designed to mimic the experience of a full\-time employee, providing an opportunity to build subject matter expertise and enhance your career at bessemer and beyond.


***primary responsibilities:***


* dissecting and understanding companies, industries, and their value chains.
* developing and sharing investment insights and delivering research conclusions.
* building and maintaining financial models including forecasts and valuation\-based screening capabilities.
* engaging with management teams, consultants, analysts, and industry experts.
* be a proactive team player, ready to assist in ad\-hoc research projects.

***qualifications:***


* actively pursuing bachelor's degree (expected graduation may 2028\).
* excellent quantitative, verbal, and written communication skills.
* advanced knowledge of microsoft office products to include outlook, power point, excel, and word.
* proficiency using factset a plus.
* ability to multi\-task and work collaboratively on a small team.


**company information and culture:**





bessemer trust is a family office, overseeing more than $250 billion in assets for over 3,000 individuals and families of substantial wealth. its more than 1,300 employees are singularly focused on private wealth management — disciplined investment management, sophisticated wealth planning, comprehensive family office services, and highly personalized client service.





established in 1907 as the family office for annie and henry phipps, bessemer trust is in its seventh generation of ownership by the phipps family. as a self\-made entrepreneur, henry phipps was a founding partner and chief financial officer of carnegie steel.





bessemer trust retains its original focus as a privately owned and independent wealth manager deeply committed to its mission of providing peace of mind to its clients. bessemer's adherence to putting clients' interests first, fiduciary mindset, and highly collaborative culture are at the heart of everything the firm does.





**key facts:**


* for 119 years, bessemer trust has operated continuously in a single line of business, independently owned by one family.
* headquartered in new york's rockefeller center, bessemer trust has 22 offices in total. woodbridge, nj, is one of the firm's largest offices, which hosts a wide range of technology and operations professionals. in addition to its sizable presence in new york and woodbridge, the firm provides client service through offices in atlanta, boston, chicago, dallas, delaware, denver, garden city, grand cayman, greenwich, houston, los angeles, miami, naples, nevada, palm beach, san diego, san francisco, seattle, stuart, and washington, d.c.
* to watch a video about bessemer trust's history, click here.
* to learn more about bessemer trust, click here.


**primary responsibilities:**


* software development: assist in the design, development, and testing of software applications. this includes coding, debugging, and collaborating with team members on software solutions.
* collaboration: work closely with cross\-functional teams including business analysts, testers and business areas directly to understand requirements and deliver high\-quality software.
* problem solving: analyze and troubleshoot software issues, identifying and implementing solutions in a timely manner.
* code reviews: participate in code reviews to ensure code quality, adherence to best practices, and continuous learning.


**qualifications:**


* education: currently pursuing a degree in computer science, software engineering, or a related field.
* programming skills: proficiency in at least one programming language such as python, java, c\#, or javascript.
* knowledge: basic understanding of software development principles, data structures, algorithms, and object\-oriented programming.
* analytical skills: strong problem\-solving skills and the ability to work with complex technical concepts.
* attention to detail: high level of accuracy and attention to detail in all work tasks.
* knowledge of one or more of the following:
* web development frameworks (e.g., react or angular).
* database systems (e.g., sql, postgresql, or mongodb).
* version control systems (e.g., git).
* cloud platforms (e.g., aws, azure) is a plus.
* experience or interest in generative ai technologies is a plus

**what we offer:**


* hands\-on experience with real\-world projects.
* mentorship and guidance from experienced software engineers.
* exposure to the financial services industry and wealth management.
* networking opportunities within the firm.


*the hourly rate for this position ranges from $35\.00 \- $40\.00\. hourly rates will vary and will be based on various factors.*


*bessemer trust is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. we encourage candidates of diverse backgrounds to apply.*
